title: Connections & Directories
description: Database connections, file paths, and server binding

settings.label: Realm ID
settings.desc: Must match the realm ID in the realmd database realmlist table. Each realm on a server needs a unique ID.

settings.label: Data Directory
settings.desc: Path to extracted client data (maps, vmaps, mmaps, DBC files). Must be quoted. Server won't start without valid data here.

settings.label: Logs Directory
settings.desc: Directory for log files. Empty string means logs go to the server's working directory. Directory must exist or all logging will fail.

settings.label: Honor Data Directory
settings.desc: Directory for weekly honor calculation result files (HCR logs). Empty means current working directory.

settings.label: Login DB Connection
settings.desc: Connection string for the login/realm database. Format: host;port;user;password;database. Used for account authentication, realm list, and ban management.

settings.label: Login DB Connections
settings.desc: Number of dedicated SELECT query connections to the login database. More connections help under high login load. Maximum 16.

settings.label: Login DB Workers
settings.desc: Async worker threads for login database operations (async SELECTs, executes, transactions). Each thread gets its own connection.

settings.label: World DB Connection
settings.desc: Connection string for the world database containing all game data — NPCs, quests, items, spells, loot tables. This is read-heavy.

settings.label: World DB Connections
settings.desc: Dedicated SELECT connections for the world database. Increase for busy servers with many simultaneous queries.

settings.label: World DB Workers
settings.desc: Async worker threads for world database operations.

settings.label: Characters DB Connection
settings.desc: Connection string for player character data — inventory, skills, quests, position, mail. This is the most write-heavy database.

settings.label: Characters DB Connections
settings.desc: Dedicated SELECT connections for the character database. Important for high-pop servers.

settings.label: Characters DB Workers
settings.desc: Async worker threads for character database saves and queries.

settings.label: Logs DB Connection
settings.desc: Connection string for the logs database — GM actions, chat, trades, battlegrounds. Can be on a separate server to reduce load.

settings.label: Logs DB Connections
settings.desc: Dedicated SELECT connections for the logs database.

settings.label: Logs DB Workers
settings.desc: Async worker threads for logs database writes.

settings.label: DB Keepalive Interval
settings.desc: Seconds between database keepalive pings. Prevents MySQL from dropping idle connections. Lower values are safer but add minor overhead.

settings.label: World Server Port
settings.desc: TCP port the world server listens on for game client connections. Must match the realmlist entry in the database.

settings.label: Bind IP Address
settings.desc: IP address to bind the world server to. 0.0.0.0 accepts connections on all interfaces. Change only if running multiple instances on different IPs.
settings.warn: Changing this incorrectly will make the server unreachable

title: Performance
description: CPU, memory, map loading, and server tick settings

settings.label: Processor Affinity
settings.desc: CPU core affinity bitmask (Windows only). 0 lets the OS decide which cores to use. On Linux this has no effect — use taskset instead.

settings.label: Process Priority
settings.desc: Process priority on Windows. 1 = High priority, 0 = Normal. On Linux this has no effect — use nice/renice instead.

settings.label: Packet Compression Level
settings.desc: Compression level for update packets sent to clients. 1 = fastest (minimal CPU, more bandwidth), 9 = best compression (more CPU, less bandwidth). For LAN servers, 1 is ideal.

settings.label: Compression Min Size
settings.desc: Maximum size in bytes of update packets allowed to be sent uncompressed. Packets larger than this will be compressed. Lower values compress more aggressively.

settings.label: Movement Compression Threshold
settings.desc: Number of movement packets within 10 seconds before compression kicks in for a session. Helps with bandwidth in crowded areas.

settings.label: Player Limit
settings.desc: Maximum players online (excluding staff). 0 = unlimited, -1 = Mods/GMs/Admins only, -2 = GMs/Admins only, -3 = Admins only. Can be overridden upward by LoginPerTick.
settings.warn: Setting to 0 on a public server means no population cap

settings.label: Player Hard Limit
settings.desc: Absolute population cap that cannot be exceeded even by LoginPerTick increases. 0 = disabled (no hard cap). Use this as a safety valve for server stability.

settings.label: Login Queue Grace Period
settings.desc: Seconds after logout during which a player can skip the login queue and rejoin immediately. 0 = disabled, players always queue. Useful for disconnects.

settings.label: Logins Per Tick
settings.desc: Maximum players let in from queue per world tick (~100ms). WARNING: This overwrites PlayerLimit upward as players join. 0 = disabled.
settings.warn: Non-zero values will increase PlayerLimit beyond its configured value

settings.label: Character Screen Idle Timeout
settings.desc: Seconds a player can sit idle at the character select screen before being disconnected. 0 = no timeout (never disconnect). Helps free slots on busy servers.

settings.label: Save Respawn Times Immediately
settings.desc: Save creature/object respawn timers to the database immediately on death/use (1), or wait until the grid unloads (0). Immediate saves are safer against crashes but slightly more DB writes.

settings.label: Max Overspeed Pings
settings.desc: How many overspeed ping responses before kicking a player. 0 = disable the check entirely. Minimum effective value is 2. Low values may cause false kicks on laggy connections.

settings.label: Grid Unload
settings.desc: Unload map grid data when no players are nearby. Saves RAM but causes brief hitches when players enter previously unloaded areas. Disable (0) if you have plenty of RAM for smoother gameplay.

settings.label: Grid Cleanup Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds before an unused grid is unloaded. 300000 = 5 minutes. Lower values free RAM faster but may cause more loading hitches if players revisit areas quickly.

settings.label: Cleanup Terrain Data
settings.desc: Clean terrain data for unloaded grids. Frees memory but costs CPU when reloading. Disable on servers with lots of RAM.

settings.label: Map Update Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between map update ticks. 100 = 10 ticks/sec (Blizzlike). Lower values make the server more responsive but use more CPU. Going below 50 is not recommended.
settings.warn: Values below 50ms can cause excessive CPU usage; values above 200ms make combat feel sluggish

settings.label: Weather Update Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between weather system updates. 600000 = 10 minutes. Weather changes (rain, snow, sandstorms) are recalculated on this interval.

settings.label: Player Auto-Save Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between automatic player data saves to the database. 900000 = 15 minutes. Lower values protect against data loss from crashes but increase DB write load.

settings.label: Stats Save Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum character level for saving stats to the character_stats table (used by websites, armory). 0 = disabled. Set to 1+ to enable for external stat display.

settings.label: Stats Save Only on Logout
settings.desc: Only save character stats on logout (1) vs every auto-save interval (0). Logout-only is better for performance, but stats shown on a website will be slightly stale.

settings.label: Preload Continent Terrain
settings.desc: Load all continent terrain data at startup. Uses significantly more RAM (~2-3 GB) but eliminates loading hitches during gameplay. Required if MapUpdate.Threads > 1 to prevent crashes. Disable on dev servers for 90% faster startup.
settings.warn: Must be enabled if using multi-threaded map updates to prevent race condition crashes

settings.label: Preload Instance Terrain
settings.desc: Load all instance/dungeon terrain at startup. Same tradeoffs as continent preloading. Required alongside continent preload when using multi-threaded map updates.

settings.label: Enable Line of Sight
settings.desc: Enable VMap-based line of sight checks. Creatures and players can't see or attack through walls/objects. Requires extracted VMaps. Disabling makes combat less realistic but saves CPU.

settings.label: Enable VMap Height
settings.desc: Use VMaps for accurate vertical positioning. Prevents creatures from spawning inside terrain or floating. Requires extracted VMaps.

settings.label: Enable Indoor Detection
settings.desc: Detect whether a player is indoors or outdoors using VMaps. Used to remove outdoor-only buffs (like mounts) when entering buildings. Requires VMaps.

settings.label: Enable Pathfinding
settings.desc: Enable Movement Map pathfinding for creatures. NPCs will navigate around obstacles, buildings, and terrain instead of walking through them. Requires extracted MMaps. Significant CPU cost but dramatically improves AI quality.

settings.label: Unload Collision Models
settings.desc: Free collision model data from memory when no players are near. Saves RAM at the cost of reloading models when players return. Disable on high-RAM servers.

settings.label: Detect Position Collision
settings.desc: Check final positions for summons, teleports, and moves against collision models. Prevents players from being placed inside walls. Uses more CPU but prevents exploits.

settings.label: Path Recalculate Range
settings.desc: Yards from target before the server recalculates a creature's chase path. Lower = more responsive chasing but more CPU. Higher = less CPU but creatures may overshoot or appear sluggish. Range: 0.5 (contact) to 5.0 (melee range).

settings.label: Uptime Update Interval
settings.desc: Minutes between updates to the 'uptime' table in the database. Used by external monitoring tools. Lower values give more granular uptime tracking.

settings.label: Max Core Stuck Time
settings.desc: Seconds before force-crashing if the server main loop freezes. 0 = disabled. Recommended > 10 seconds if enabled. Useful for production servers to auto-restart via systemd after a freeze.

settings.label: Ban List Reload Interval
settings.desc: Seconds between reloading IP and account ban lists from the database. Useful when multiple realm servers share a login database — bans applied on one realm take effect on others after this interval.

settings.label: Allow Addon Channel
settings.desc: Allow the addon communication channel. Most popular addons (Questie, DPSMeter, etc.) require this to function. Disabling breaks nearly all addons that communicate between players.
settings.warn: Disabling will break most popular addons

settings.label: Clean Character DB on Start
settings.desc: Run character database cleanup on startup — removes orphaned data like items belonging to deleted characters, stale auction entries, etc. Slightly slows startup but keeps the database tidy.

settings.label: Reusable GUID Pool Size
settings.desc: Number of unused GUIDs preloaded from the database for reuse when creating new creatures, items, and corpses. Larger pools reduce DB queries but use more memory at startup.

title: Map Threading
description: Multi-threaded map updates and dynamic distance scaling

settings.label: Instance Continents
settings.desc: Create separate instances for each continent (experimental). May help with multi-threaded performance but is not well tested.
settings.warn: Experimental feature — not recommended for production

settings.label: Skip Inactive Player Updates
settings.desc: Skip AI and world updates for players on continents with no active creatures nearby. Can improve performance on large maps with spread-out players.

settings.label: Reduce Grid Distance Threshold
settings.desc: Server tick diff (ms) that triggers automatic grid activation distance reduction. When server is under heavy load, this shrinks the area around players that stays active. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Increase Grid Distance Threshold
settings.desc: Server tick diff (ms) below which grid activation distance is restored to normal. Works with ReduceGridActivationDist to dynamically scale based on load. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Min Grid Activation Distance
settings.desc: Floor for dynamic grid activation distance reduction. Prevents distance from shrinking below this value even under extreme load.

settings.label: Reduce Visibility Threshold
settings.desc: Server tick diff (ms) that triggers automatic visibility distance reduction. Players will see fewer distant objects/players when the server is lagging. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Increase Visibility Threshold
settings.desc: Server tick diff (ms) below which visibility distance is restored. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Min Visibility Distance
settings.desc: Floor for dynamic visibility distance reduction. Even under heavy load, visibility won't drop below this value.

settings.label: Empty Map Update Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds after which maps with no players stop being updated. Saves CPU by pausing AI, respawns, and events on empty maps. 0 = always update all maps.

settings.label: Instance Update Threads
settings.desc: Number of threads for updating instanced maps (dungeons, raids). Each instance map can be updated in parallel. 0 = single-threaded. Increase for servers with many active instances.

settings.label: Object Update Threads
settings.desc: Maximum threads for object update processing on continent maps. These handle NPC AI ticks, spawns, and gameobject state changes. 0 = single-threaded.

settings.label: Object Update Timeout
settings.desc: Milliseconds to wait for object update threads to complete before proceeding. If threads take longer, updates may be deferred to the next tick.

settings.label: Visibility Update Threads
settings.desc: Maximum threads for visibility update processing. Handles which objects/players each client can see. 0 = single-threaded.

settings.label: Visibility Update Timeout
settings.desc: Milliseconds to wait for visibility update threads to complete.

settings.label: Packet Update Diff Threshold
settings.desc: Diff threshold (ms) before packet updates are offloaded to separate threads. Helps when the main thread is overloaded.

settings.label: Player Update Diff Threshold
settings.desc: Diff threshold (ms) before player updates are offloaded to separate threads.

settings.label: Cell Update Diff Threshold
settings.desc: Diff threshold (ms) before cell updates are offloaded to separate threads.

settings.label: Continent Cell Threads
settings.desc: Number of threads for parallel cell updates on continent maps. Cells within SafeDistance of each other won't update simultaneously (prevents race conditions). 0 = single-threaded.

settings.label: Cell Thread Safe Distance
settings.desc: Minimum distance between cells that can be updated simultaneously by multi-threaded cell processing. Lower values allow more parallelism but risk thread-safety issues.

settings.label: Creature Motion Threads
settings.desc: Threads for NPC/creature motion (pathfinding, movement) updates. 0 = disabled (motion updates on main thread). Increasing helps with many moving NPCs.

settings.label: Async Task Threads
settings.desc: Threads for asynchronous background tasks like /who queries and auction house item listing. More threads help responsiveness on busy servers.

settings.label: Async Query Tick Timeout
settings.desc: Maximum milliseconds to spend processing async query results per tick. 0 = process all available results (no limit). Setting a limit prevents query result floods from stalling the main loop.

title: Movement
description: Player and pet movement extrapolation and pathing

settings.label: Extrapolate Charge Position
settings.desc: Smooth out player position during charge abilities (Warrior Charge, etc.) by extrapolating movement. Reduces rubber-banding during charges. Not fully stable.

settings.label: Extrapolate Pet Position
settings.desc: Smooth pet following by extrapolating position. Makes pets appear to move more naturally. Not fully stable.

settings.label: Relocation VMap Check Delay
settings.desc: Delay in milliseconds between VMap collision checks during movement relocation (mount indoors, swimming detection). 0 = check every time. Higher values reduce CPU but may allow brief exploits.

settings.label: Max Waypoints Per Packet
settings.desc: Maximum movement waypoints allowed in a single client packet. Higher values allow smoother long-distance paths but increase packet sizes.

settings.label: Pending Ack Response Time
settings.desc: Milliseconds to wait for a client to acknowledge a forced movement change (speed change, teleport, root). If the client doesn't respond in time, anticheat may flag it.

title: Crash Recovery
description: Anticrash protection and recovery

settings.label: Anticrash Options
settings.desc: Bitmask for crash recovery behavior: 1=Announce crash to players, 2=Save all player data, 4=Crash instances (isolate), 8=Crash continents (isolate), 16=Create coredump. Default 28 = save+crash instances+crash continents.

settings.label: Anticrash Rearm Timer
settings.desc: Milliseconds between crashes before anticrash protection re-engages. If two crashes happen within this window, the second one is not protected. 0 = disable automatic re-arming.

title: Progression & PvP
description: Content patch, PvP honor system, and progressive settings

settings.label: Content Patch
settings.desc: Which content patch the server emulates. Controls quest/NPC/dungeon/raid availability, item stats, and game mechanics. 0=Patch 1.2 through 10=Patch 1.12. This is the master knob for progressive content release.

settings.label: Accurate PvP Equip Requirements
settings.desc: Enforce Blizzlike PvP rank requirements to equip PvP gear before Patch 1.6. When enabled, players must hold the required rank to wear PvP items, matching the original game's progression.

settings.label: Accurate PvP Purchase Requirements
settings.desc: Enforce Blizzlike PvP rank requirements to purchase gear. When enabled, players need their current (not lifetime) rank to buy PvP items from vendors.

settings.label: Accurate PvP Timeline
settings.desc: Enforce accurate PvP system timeline. The honor system didn't exist before Patch 1.4 — when enabled, players can't gain PvP rank until the appropriate patch is set.

settings.label: Accurate PvP Rewards
settings.desc: Scale PvP reputation and honorable kill rewards based on the current content patch. Reward values changed several times during vanilla.

settings.label: Dishonorable Kills
settings.desc: Give dishonorable kills (DKs) to players who kill civilian NPCs. DKs reduce PvP rank and discourage killing quest givers and vendors in enemy cities.

settings.label: City Protector
settings.desc: Award the City Protector title to the highest-ranking PvP player per faction during honor updates. This was a rare vanilla feature.

settings.label: Restore Deleted Items
settings.desc: When progressing to a new patch, automatically mail items back to players that were previously deleted because they didn't exist on the earlier patch.

settings.label: Accurate Pet Stats
settings.desc: Hunter pet damage, speed, and resistances follow patch-accurate progression. Pet stats changed across vanilla patches.

settings.label: Accurate LFG Availability
settings.desc: Disable the LFG (Looking For Group) tool before Patch 1.3 when it was introduced. Only matters for servers starting at early patches.

settings.label: Accurate PvE Events
settings.desc: Apply patch-appropriate changes to PvE scripted events. Affects quest sequences and scripted scenes that changed between patches.

settings.label: No Respec Price Decay
settings.desc: Prevent talent respec cost from decaying over time until Patch 1.11 (when monthly cost reduction was added). Keeps respec cost at its peak value on earlier patches.

settings.label: No Quest XP to Gold
settings.desc: Don't convert quest XP to gold at max level until Patch 1.10 when the feature was added. On earlier patches, completing quests at level 60 gives nothing extra.

settings.label: Unlinked Auction Houses
settings.desc: Keep Alliance and Horde auction houses separate until Patch 1.9 when faction AHs were linked. Goblin AHs (neutral) are always separate. Major impact on server economy.

settings.label: Debuff Limit
settings.desc: Maximum debuffs on a target. 0 = auto-detect based on WowPatch (8 before 1.7, 16 after). Directly affects raid strategy — with 8 debuff slots, raid leaders must carefully choose which DoTs and debuffs to apply.

title: Server Logging
description: Log files, database logging, and performance monitoring

settings.label: Log GM SQL Commands
settings.desc: Log all GM SQL commands to daily files (YYYY-MM-DD_logSQL.sql). Essential for auditing GM actions. New file created each day, old files preserved.

settings.label: PID File Path
settings.desc: Path for the process ID file. Used by init scripts and monitoring to track the running process. Empty = don't create. Recommended: './worldd.pid'.

settings.label: Console Log Level
settings.desc: Console log verbosity. 0=Errors only, 1=Minimum, 2=Basic (recommended), 3=Detail, 4=Full debug. Higher levels flood the console but help diagnose issues.

settings.label: File Log Level
settings.desc: File log verbosity. Same scale as console. Level 4 generates massive log files — only use for active debugging.

settings.label: Database Log Level
settings.desc: Database operation logging level. Higher values log individual SQL queries which is useful for debugging but very verbose.

settings.label: Console Timestamps
settings.desc: Include timestamps [HH:MM:SS] in console output. Useful for correlating events but adds visual clutter.

settings.label: Filter Transport Moves
settings.desc: Filter transport movement messages from logs. 1=hide, 0=show. Transport moves are very verbose.

settings.label: Filter Creature Moves
settings.desc: Filter creature movement messages from logs. Very verbose when many NPCs are moving.

settings.label: Filter Visibility Changes
settings.desc: Filter visibility change messages. Verbose in crowded areas.

settings.label: Filter Weather
settings.desc: Filter weather update messages from logs.

settings.label: Filter Player Stats
settings.desc: Filter player stat calculation messages. Useful for debugging stat issues.

settings.label: Filter SQL Text
settings.desc: Filter raw SQL query text from logs. Enable to reduce log size; disable to debug database issues.

settings.label: Filter Player Moves
settings.desc: Filter player movement messages. Extremely verbose if disabled.

settings.label: Filter Periodic Effects
settings.desc: Filter periodic aura tick messages (DoTs, HoTs). Very verbose in combat.

settings.label: Filter AI & Movement
settings.desc: Filter AI decision and movement generator messages. Useful for debugging NPC behavior.

settings.label: Filter Damage
settings.desc: Filter damage calculation messages. Useful for debugging combat formulas.

settings.label: Filter Combat
settings.desc: Filter general combat messages from logs.

settings.label: Filter Spell Casts
settings.desc: Filter spell cast messages. Very verbose during combat.

settings.label: Filter DB Strict Checks
settings.desc: Filter database strictness check messages.

settings.label: Filter Pathfinding
settings.desc: Filter pathfinding/mmap calculation messages. Useful for debugging NPC movement issues.

settings.label: Filter Honor
settings.desc: Filter honor calculation messages. Can be verbose during honor updates.

settings.label: Timestamp Log Filenames
settings.desc: Append startup timestamp to log filenames (Server_2025-04-04_12-00-00.log). Prevents log files from being overwritten on restart.

settings.label: Server Log File
settings.desc: Main server log file. Empty string disables file logging entirely. Contains general server operations, warnings, and errors.

settings.label: Anticheat Log File
settings.desc: Log file for anticheat detection results. Empty = disabled. Review this file to tune anticheat sensitivity.

settings.label: Scripts Log File
settings.desc: Log file for script engine errors and debug output. Essential for diagnosing broken quests and events.

settings.label: Movement Log File
settings.desc: Log file for movement system errors and debug info. Useful for diagnosing rubber-banding and stuck NPCs.

settings.label: Chat Log File
settings.desc: Player chat log file. Records all say, yell, whisper, and channel messages. Essential for moderation. Empty = disabled.

settings.label: Battleground Log File
settings.desc: Log file for battleground system events — queue, start, end, rewards.

settings.label: Character Log File
settings.desc: Character operations log — creation, deletion, rename. CharLogDump can add full character dumps before deletion for restoration.

settings.label: Honor Log File
settings.desc: Honor calculation log file. Empty = disabled. Enable to debug honor distribution issues.

settings.label: RA Log File
settings.desc: Remote Administration command log. Records all commands sent via RA (including MangosSuperUI commands). Important for security auditing.

settings.label: DB Errors Log File
settings.desc: Log file for database errors detected during server operation. Review regularly for data integrity issues.

settings.label: DB Error Fix File
settings.desc: SQL file containing auto-generated fix queries for detected DB errors. Empty = disabled. Enable to get ready-to-apply patches.

settings.label: Loot Log File
settings.desc: Loot event log file — who looted what from which creature. Useful for investigating loot disputes.

settings.label: Level Up Log File
settings.desc: Level-up event log — tracks which characters leveled and when.

settings.label: Performance Log File
settings.desc: Performance metrics log — slow updates, lag spikes, thread timeouts. Critical for production monitoring.

settings.label: GM Commands Log File
settings.desc: GM command log file. Empty = disabled. Enable on production servers for accountability. Separate from the SQL-based GM logging.

settings.label: GM Critical Log File
settings.desc: Log for critical GM commands used on the GM's own character (same IP). Flags potential self-abuse of GM powers.

settings.label: Trades Log File
settings.desc: Trade log — records player-to-player trades including items and gold exchanged.

settings.label: Network Log File
settings.desc: Network events log — connection issues, packet errors, disconnects.

settings.label: Money Log Threshold
settings.desc: Log all trades where money exchanged exceeds this amount in copper. 10000 = 1 gold. Helps detect suspicious gold transfers.

settings.label: Character Dump on Delete
settings.desc: Include full character data dump in Char.log before deletion. Enables manual character restoration by cutting the dump from the log and using loadpdump.

settings.label: Separate GM Logs Per Account
settings.desc: Create separate GM log files per account (Logname_#AccountID.ext) instead of one shared file. Easier to review individual GM actions.

settings.label: DB Log: GM Commands
settings.desc: Log GM commands to the database (in addition to file logging). Enables querying GM actions via SQL.

settings.label: DB Log: Chat
settings.desc: Log player chat to the database. Enables searching chat history via SQL. Can generate significant DB writes on busy servers.

settings.label: DB Log: Characters
settings.desc: Log character events (create, delete, rename) to the database.

settings.label: DB Log: Level Ups
settings.desc: Log level-up events to the database. Useful for tracking leveling progression.

settings.label: DB Log: Loot
settings.desc: Log loot events to the database. Can generate very high DB write volume.

settings.label: DB Log: Trades
settings.desc: Log player trades to the database. Important for investigating scams and disputes.

settings.label: DB Log: Transactions
settings.desc: Log gold transactions (vendor buys/sells, mail, AH) to the database.

settings.label: DB Log: Battlegrounds
settings.desc: Log battleground events and results to the database.

settings.label: Smart Log: Deaths
settings.desc: Enable smart logging for player deaths — captures context around death events for debugging.

settings.label: Smart Log: Long Combat
settings.desc: Enable smart logging for long combat encounters — captures extended fights for analysis.

settings.label: Long Combat Duration
settings.desc: Seconds threshold for 'long combat' smart logging. 1800 = 30 minutes. Fights longer than this get extra logging.

settings.label: Slow World Update Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when world update takes longer than this (ms). 0 = disabled. Helps identify server performance issues.

settings.label: Slow Map System Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when map system update exceeds this threshold (ms).

settings.label: Slow Sessions Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when all sessions update takes too long (ms).

settings.label: Slow Session Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when a single player session update exceeds this (ms). Helps find problematic players/addons.

settings.label: Slow Map Update Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when a single map update exceeds this (ms).

settings.label: Slow Async Query Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when async database queries take too long (ms).

settings.label: Slow Packets Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when packet processing exceeds this (ms).

settings.label: Slow Map Packets Threshold
settings.desc: Log warning when map packet processing exceeds this (ms).

settings.label: Slow Packet Broadcast
settings.desc: Log warning when packet broadcast exceeds this (ms). 0 = disabled.

title: Server Settings
description: Game rules, player limits, instances, quests, mail, and miscellaneous

settings.label: Realm Type
settings.desc: Realm type shown in the server list. 0/4=Normal (PvE), 1=PvP, 6=RP, 8=RP-PvP, 16=FFA PvP (arena-style free-for-all everywhere except sanctuaries). Affects PvP flagging rules.

settings.label: Realm Zone
settings.desc: Server region setting. Controls allowed character name alphabets and client language. 1=Development (any), 2=US, 8=English, 9=German, 10=French, 12=Russian. Wrong values can hide the realm from certain clients.
settings.warn: Changing this may make the realm invisible to some game clients

settings.label: Timezone Offset
settings.desc: Hours offset from UTC for server time. Affects in-game time display and scheduled events.

settings.label: DBC Locale
settings.desc: Language for DBC data files. 0=English, 1=Korean, 2=French, 3=German, 4=Chinese, 5=Taiwanese, 6=Spanish, 255=Auto-detect from installed DBC files.

settings.label: Declined Names
settings.desc: Enable declined name forms (Russian localization feature for grammatically correct name cases).

settings.label: Strict Player Names
settings.desc: Character name validation strictness. 0=OS-level only, 1=Latin characters only, 2=RealmZone specific, 3=Latin + zone specific. Stricter values flag existing invalid names for rename.

settings.label: Strict Charter Names
settings.desc: Guild charter name validation. Same rules as StrictPlayerNames.

settings.label: Strict Pet Names
settings.desc: Pet name validation. Same rules as StrictPlayerNames.

settings.label: Min Player Name Length
settings.desc: Minimum character name length. 2 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: Min Charter Name Length
settings.desc: Minimum guild name length. 2 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: Min Pet Name Length
settings.desc: Minimum pet name length. 2 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: World Available
settings.desc: Whether the world accepts player logins. 0 = maintenance mode (character creation still works but world login blocked). Useful for server maintenance without shutting down.

settings.label: Character Creation Disabled
settings.desc: Disable character creation. 0=enabled, 1=Alliance disabled, 2=Horde disabled, 3=both disabled. Useful for faction balance or maintenance.

settings.label: Characters Per Account
settings.desc: Maximum total characters across ALL realms per account. 50 is the Blizzlike cap.

settings.label: Characters Per Realm
settings.desc: Maximum characters per realm per account. 10 is the client-enforced maximum.

settings.label: Limit Play Time
settings.desc: Chinese anti-addiction system. After 3 hours: gold/XP halved. After 5 hours: gold/XP stopped. Only used on Chinese servers.

settings.label: Restrict Unverified Accounts
settings.desc: Apply trial-like restrictions to accounts without verified email: no guild, limited whispers, no trade, blocked from public channels, level cap 20, gold cap 10g, profession cap 100/150.

settings.label: Skip Cinematics
settings.desc: Skip race intro cinematics on first login. 0=Show all, 1=Skip all. Useful with custom start locations where cinematics may be buggy.

settings.label: Show Health Values
settings.desc: Send actual numeric health values to clients instead of percentages. Client needs an addon to display them. Non-Blizzlike but popular on private servers.

settings.label: Max Player Level
settings.desc: Maximum level a player can reach. 60 is Blizzlike for vanilla. Changing this affects the entire game balance — XP curves, quest availability, dungeon scaling, and PvP brackets.
settings.warn: Changing from 60 will break many aspects of vanilla game balance

settings.label: Starting Level
settings.desc: Starting level for newly created characters. 1 is Blizzlike. Higher values let players skip early content. Must be ≤ MaxPlayerLevel.

settings.label: Starting Money
settings.desc: Starting copper for new characters. 0 = Blizzlike (no starting money). 10000 = 1 gold. Giving starting gold can help new players but may affect server economy.

settings.label: Min Honor Kills for Ranking
settings.desc: Minimum honorable kills per week to participate in honor calculations. 0 = auto-select based on current patch. Historically was 15 kills.

settings.label: Rank Point Decay Rate
settings.desc: Weekly rank point decay rate. 0.2 = 20% (Blizzlike). Lower values make ranking easier/faster. 0 = no decay, players never lose rank progress. Major impact on how long it takes to reach Rank 14.

settings.label: Maintenance Day
settings.desc: Day of the week for server maintenance and honor distribution. 0=Sunday through 6=Saturday. 3=Wednesday (Blizzlike EU). Honor standings reset and ranks update on this day.

settings.label: Instant Logout Level
settings.desc: Minimum security level for instant logout (skip 20-second countdown). 0=All players, 1=Mods+, 2=GMs+. Normal players always wait 20 seconds unless in an inn/city.

settings.label: Force Logout Delay
settings.desc: Keep player characters in-game briefly after unexpected disconnect. Prevents combat-logging (alt-F4 to escape PvP death). 1=enabled, 0=disabled.

settings.label: Auto Honor Restart
settings.desc: Automatically schedule a server restart when it's time for PvP rank calculations. Essential for consistent honor updates.

settings.label: All Flight Paths
settings.desc: All players start with every flight path (both factions). 0 = must discover (Blizzlike). 1 = all unlocked. Makes travel much easier but removes exploration incentive.

settings.label: Auto-Max Skills
settings.desc: Weapon and defense skills always stay maxed for current level. 0 = must level skills by using them (Blizzlike). 1 = auto-maxed. Removes weapon skill grinding.

settings.label: Enable Weather
settings.desc: Enable dynamic weather (rain, snow, sandstorms) in outdoor zones. Weather is purely cosmetic in vanilla but adds atmosphere.

settings.label: Allow Unstuck
settings.desc: Allow players to use the Unstuck feature via the Help menu or .start command. Teleports stuck players to their hearthstone location.

settings.label: Max Spell Chain Casts
settings.desc: Maximum triggered spell casts in a chain before breaking. Prevents infinite proc loops from causing stack overflow crashes. 0 = no limit (dangerous).

settings.label: Ignore Instance Level
settings.desc: Skip level requirements to enter instances. 0 = enforce (Blizzlike). 1 = any level can enter any dungeon.

settings.label: Ignore Raid Requirement
settings.desc: Skip raid group requirement for raid instances. 0 = must be in raid (Blizzlike). 1 = any group size can enter raids.

settings.label: Instance Reset Hour
settings.desc: Hour of the day (0-23) for global instance resets. 4 = 4:00 AM server time. Choose a low-activity hour.

settings.label: Instance Unload Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds before unloading an empty instance map. 1800000 = 30 minutes. 0 = keep in memory until reset. Freeing memory helps with many instances.

settings.label: Instances Per Hour
settings.desc: Maximum dungeon instances a player can enter per hour. 5 is Blizzlike (added to combat instance farming). 0 = no limit. Affects gold farming and dungeon grinding efficiency.

settings.label: Instant Save Item Quality
settings.desc: Item quality threshold for instant database save on acquisition. 0=Poor through 6=Artifact. Lower values save more items instantly (safer against crashes) but increase DB writes.

settings.label: Prevent Item Data Mining
settings.desc: Don't send item data to clients for items that have never been obtained. Prevents datamining unreleased items. Important for progressive servers.

settings.label: Low Quest Hide Diff
settings.desc: Hide quest markers for quests this many levels below the player. -1 = show all quests. 4 = quests 5+ levels below are hidden (Blizzlike). Affects quest giver exclamation marks only.

settings.label: High Quest Hide Diff
settings.desc: Hide quest markers for quests requiring a level this far above the player. -1 = show all. 7 = quests requiring 8+ levels above are hidden.

settings.label: Quests Ignore Raid
settings.desc: Allow turning in non-raid quests while in a raid group. 0 = blocked (Blizzlike). 1 = allowed. Convenience feature.

settings.label: Offline Leader Timeout
settings.desc: Seconds before leadership transfers from an offline group leader. 300 = 5 minutes grace period. 0 = never auto-transfer (group stuck until leader returns).

settings.label: Guild Event Log Size
settings.desc: Maximum guild event log entries stored. Higher values preserve more history but slightly impact DB performance. Oldest events are overwritten when full.

settings.label: Fatigue Timer
settings.desc: Seconds of fatigue meter when swimming past map edges before drowning. 60 = Blizzlike (1 minute). 0 = instant death at boundary. Controls how far players can swim into the void.

settings.label: Breath Timer
settings.desc: Seconds of breath when underwater before drowning damage starts. 60 = Blizzlike (1 minute). 0 = instant drowning.

settings.label: Environmental Damage Delay
settings.desc: Seconds delay before environmental damage (lava) starts ticking. 1 = near-instant.

settings.label: Environmental Damage Min
settings.desc: Minimum environmental damage per tick (lava/slime). 605 is Blizzlike.

settings.label: Environmental Damage Max
settings.desc: Maximum environmental damage per tick. Actual damage randomized between Min and Max.

settings.label: Max Primary Trade Skills
settings.desc: Maximum primary professions per character. 2 is Blizzlike. Increasing allows more self-sufficiency but reduces inter-player trade dependency.

settings.label: Guild Petition Signatures
settings.desc: Signatures needed on a guild charter. 9 is Blizzlike. Set to 0 for solo guild creation (useful on low-pop servers or for bots).

settings.label: Group XP Distance
settings.desc: Maximum yards from a creature kill for group members to receive XP. 74 is Blizzlike. Players further away get nothing. Affects dungeon strategy (out-of-range players miss XP).

settings.label: Mail Delivery Delay
settings.desc: Seconds before item-containing mail arrives. 3600 = 1 hour (Blizzlike). 0 = instant delivery. Longer delays slow down the economy and discourage mailing as fast-transfer.

settings.label: COD Force Tag Max Level
settings.desc: Add 'COD' prefix to COD mail subjects from characters below this level. Prevents COD scam attempts by making the cost obvious. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Mass Mail Per Tick
settings.desc: Maximum mails processed per tick during mass mailings (GM announcements, event rewards). Higher values process mass mail faster but can stall the server.

settings.label: Returned Mail Per Tick
settings.desc: Maximum expired mails returned/deleted per tick. Runs every 30 minutes. Low values can cause backlog if many mails expire at once.

settings.label: Unsummon Pet on Mount
settings.desc: Unsummon permanent pets when mounting. 0 = smart (only unsummon when needed). 1 = always unsummon all pets on mount.

settings.label: Default Pet Loyalty
settings.desc: Starting loyalty level for newly tamed hunter pets. 1 = Blizzlike (starts at lowest). Higher values skip the loyalty grind.

settings.label: Player Chat Commands
settings.desc: Parse player chat messages for dot-commands (.start, .help, etc.). 0 = ignore all commands from players (commands only work in console/RA).

settings.label: Announce Events
settings.desc: Broadcast a system message to all players when a game event starts (holidays, fishing tournaments, etc.).

settings.label: Auto Broadcast Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between auto-broadcast messages (defined in the database). 1800000 = 30 minutes.

settings.label: Spell Effect Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds before most spell effects are processed after cast completes. This is the vanilla 'spell batching' window — two players can simultaneously kill each other, or a spell can land after the caster dies. 400ms is Blizzlike.

settings.label: Spell Proc Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds before spell procs are processed. Doubled internally for aura-triggered procs to simulate Blizzlike batching behavior. Affects how quickly Windfury, Reckoning, and similar procs trigger.

settings.label: Beep at Startup
settings.desc: Play a beep sound when the server finishes loading. Useful as an audible notification that startup is complete.

settings.label: Show Progress Bars
settings.desc: Show progress bars during server startup while loading data tables. Cosmetic only.

settings.label: Startup Error Wait
settings.desc: Seconds to wait after a startup error before continuing. -1 = wait for Enter key. 0 = no wait. Gives you time to read error messages before the window closes.

settings.label: Message of the Day
settings.desc: Message of the Day shown to every player on login. Use @ for line breaks. Good for announcements, rules, or server info. Example: 'Welcome to Barrens Chat!@Type .help for commands.'

title: Cross-Faction Interaction
description: Allow or restrict interactions between Alliance and Horde

settings.label: Allow Both Factions Per Account
settings.desc: Allow accounts to have characters on both Alliance and Horde. 0 = faction-locked (Blizzlike on PvP realms). Enabling allows potential spy alts and cross-faction coordination.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Chat
settings.desc: Allow Alliance and Horde players to understand each other in /say and /yell. 0 = language barrier (Blizzlike). Enabling removes a core faction-war immersion element.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Channels
settings.desc: Allow shared chat channels (General, Trade, LFG) across factions. 0 = separate channels (Blizzlike).

settings.label: GM Cross-Faction Channels
settings.desc: Allow GM characters to join and monitor opposite faction chat channels. Useful for moderation on both sides.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Groups
settings.desc: Allow cross-faction grouping for dungeons and quests. Dramatically changes the game dynamic on low-pop servers.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Guilds
settings.desc: Allow guilds with members from both factions. Non-Blizzlike but popular on roleplay and low-pop servers.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Trading
settings.desc: Allow direct trading between Alliance and Horde players. Non-Blizzlike but useful on low-pop servers.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Auction House
settings.desc: Merge Alliance and Horde auction houses into one. Doubles the buyer/seller pool but removes faction-based economic competition.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Mail
settings.desc: Allow sending mail between Alliance and Horde characters. Non-Blizzlike.

settings.label: Cross-Faction /who
settings.desc: Show both factions in /who results. Lets players see the total server population.

settings.label: Cross-Faction Friends
settings.desc: Allow adding opposite-faction players to friends list. Useful for cross-faction communities.

title: Creatures & World Objects
description: Aggro, corpse decay, damage scaling, and chat ranges

settings.label: Threat/Evade Radius
settings.desc: Maximum yards from spawn point before a creature evades (resets, runs back, heals to full). 50 is Blizzlike. Higher values let players kite mobs further. Lower values make kiting harder.

settings.label: Aggro Range Multiplier
settings.desc: Multiplier for creature aggro radius. 1.0 = Blizzlike. 0 = creatures never aggro (peaceful mode). 2.0 = double aggro range (much more dangerous world). Affects the feel of open-world travel significantly.

settings.label: Max Aggro Distance
settings.desc: Maximum yards a creature will search for targets. Lower values improve server performance but may make creatures less responsive to nearby threats.

settings.label: Player Stealth Detect Range
settings.desc: Maximum range players can detect stealthed units. Affects rogue/druid PvP balance.

settings.label: Creature Stealth Detect Range
settings.desc: Maximum range creatures can detect stealthed players. Lower values make stealth more powerful.

settings.label: Max Summon Limit
settings.desc: Maximum creatures that can be summoned by one source (spell, game object). Prevents runaway spawns from crashing the server.

settings.label: Flee Assistance Radius
settings.desc: Yards a fleeing creature searches for allies. 0 = disabled (creatures flee aimlessly). Higher values make enemies more likely to bring friends.

settings.label: Call for Help Radius
settings.desc: Yards for 'call for help' aggro — nearby same-family creatures will assist. 0 = disabled. 10 is Blizzlike. Higher values make pulls more dangerous.

settings.label: Help Response Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds before nearby creatures respond to a call for help. 1500 = 1.5 seconds reaction time.

settings.label: Flee Duration
settings.desc: Milliseconds a creature flees before stopping (if no help found). 7000 = 7 seconds.

settings.label: World Boss Level Diff
settings.desc: Level difference for world boss skull classification. 3 = bosses appear as skull (level ??) if 3+ levels above player.

settings.label: Normal Corpse Decay
settings.desc: Seconds before normal creature corpses despawn (if unlooted). 300 = 5 minutes.

settings.label: Rare Corpse Decay
settings.desc: Rare creature corpse decay time. 900 = 15 minutes. Longer because rares are hard to find.

settings.label: Elite Corpse Decay
settings.desc: Elite creature corpse decay time. 600 = 10 minutes.

settings.label: Rare-Elite Corpse Decay
settings.desc: Rare-elite corpse decay time. 1200 = 20 minutes.

settings.label: World Boss Corpse Decay
settings.desc: World boss corpse decay time. 3600 = 1 hour. Long so raiders have time to distribute loot.

settings.label: Looted Corpse Decay Rate
settings.desc: Decay time multiplier for looted corpses. 0.0 = use spawntimesecs/3 as decay. Values like 0.1 make looted corpses disappear faster.

settings.label: Resend Loot Rolls on Reconnect
settings.desc: Resend active loot roll windows when a player reconnects. 1 = resend (player doesn't miss rolls on DC).

settings.label: /say Range
settings.desc: Yards that /say messages travel. 25 is Blizzlike (approximately the player's immediate vicinity). Your server currently has this at 40.

settings.label: /emote Range
settings.desc: Yards that /me emote messages travel. 25 is Blizzlike.

settings.label: /yell Range
settings.desc: Yards that /yell messages travel. 300 is Blizzlike (roughly the visible area). Affects how far players can communicate in open world.

settings.label: Creature GUID Reserve
settings.desc: GUIDs reserved for creature spawns via .npc add. Determines how many creatures can be spawned before restart. 1000 gives ample room.

settings.label: Object GUID Reserve
settings.desc: GUIDs reserved for game object spawns via .gobject add.

title: Chat Settings
description: Chat restrictions, flood protection, channel permissions, and level requirements

settings.label: Block Fake Messages
settings.desc: Block chat messages that abuse invisible characters and excessive spaces to create fake system messages or impersonate other players. May break some old addons that use normal chat channels for data transfer.

settings.label: Link Check Severity
settings.desc: Validate item/spell links in chat messages. 0=Off, 1=Check for valid pipe commands (blocks image injection), 2=Verify correct command ordering, 3=Full validation including color/entry/name consistency (requires all locale DBCs).

settings.label: Kick on Bad Links
settings.desc: Action on failed link check. 0 = silently block the message. 1 = kick the player. Kicking may be too aggressive for false positives.

settings.label: Flood Message Count
settings.desc: Maximum messages a player can send within the flood detection window before being muted. 0 = disable flood protection. 10 is a reasonable default — low enough to catch spammers, high enough for normal conversation.

settings.label: Flood Time Window
settings.desc: Seconds of the flood detection window. Messages sent faster than this interval count toward the flood limit. 1 second is Blizzlike.

settings.label: Flood Mute Duration
settings.desc: Seconds a player is muted after triggering flood protection. 10 is brief enough to not frustrate legitimate players but long enough to stop spam bursts.

settings.label: Silent GM Channel Join
settings.desc: GMs join channels without the '[Player] has joined' announcement. 1 = silent join. Useful for covert moderation.

settings.label: Latin Only in General
settings.desc: Block non-Latin characters in General channels. 1 = Latin only. May frustrate players on international servers.

settings.label: World Channel Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum character level to use World channels. Raising this prevents brand-new characters from spamming world chat (anti-gold-seller measure).

settings.label: World Channel Cooldown
settings.desc: Seconds between messages on world channels. 0 = no cooldown. Useful for slowing down spam in World/LFG channels without fully blocking access.

settings.label: World Cooldown Bypass Level
settings.desc: Level at which world channel cooldown is removed. 0 = no level-based cooldown bypass.

settings.label: World Cooldown Level Scaling
settings.desc: Scale world channel cooldown based on player level. Lower level = longer cooldown. 0 = no scaling.

settings.label: Cross-Zone Whisper Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum level to whisper players in different zones. Prevents freshly created characters from whisper-spamming gold selling ads across zones.

settings.label: Yell Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum character level to use /yell. 0 = no restriction. Raising this prevents low-level spam bots from yelling advertisements.

settings.label: Yell Range Linear Scale
settings.desc: Linearly scale yell range based on level up to this max. 0 = off (full range for everyone).

settings.label: Yell Range Quadratic Scale
settings.desc: Quadratically scale yell range by level. 0 = off.

settings.label: Channel Invite Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum level to invite other players into private chat channels. 10 is a reasonable anti-spam measure. 0 = anyone can invite.

settings.label: Public Channel Bypass Level
settings.desc: Character level required to bypass ALL public channel restrictions (General, Trade, LookingForGroup, LocalDefense). On a max-60 server, setting this to 61 means NO player can ever bypass restrictions. Set to 0 to disable all level-based channel restrictions. Set to 1-60 to require a minimum level.
settings.warn: Value 61+ on a max-60 server blocks ALL players from public channels like General and Trade. Set to 0 to disable.

settings.label: Whisper Block Command
settings.desc: Enable the .wr command allowing players to block incoming whispers. Useful for players harassed by gold sellers.

settings.label: Say Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum character level to use /say in local chat. 0 = no restriction. Raising this prevents level-1 spam bots from talking in cities.

settings.label: Emote Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum character level to use /me emotes. 0 = no restriction.

settings.label: Anti-Flood Penalty
settings.desc: Bitmask penalty for sending too many packets. 1=Log, 2=Report to GM, 4=Announce, 8=Kick, 16=Ban. Default 8 = kick. Use additive values: 11 = Log+Report+Kick.

title: Warden Anticheat
description: Client memory scanning and cheat detection

settings.label: Windows Warden
settings.desc: Enable Warden anti-cheat memory scanning for Windows clients. Detects modified game files, known cheat programs, and unauthorized memory modifications. Requires warden module files.

settings.label: macOS Warden
settings.desc: Enable Warden scanning for macOS clients.

settings.label: Skip GM Checks
settings.desc: Skip Warden checks for GM accounts. 1 = GMs are exempt from Warden scanning.

settings.label: Scans Per Cycle
settings.desc: Number of memory scan checks sent to the client each cycle. More scans = better cheat detection but more network traffic and client CPU usage.

settings.label: Client Response Timeout
settings.desc: Seconds to wait for Warden scan response before disconnecting the client. 0 = never disconnect (logging only). Too low may kick players on slow connections.

settings.label: Scan Frequency
settings.desc: Seconds between Warden scan cycles. Lower = more frequent scanning (better detection, more load). Higher = less intrusive but slower to catch cheats.

settings.label: Default Penalty
settings.desc: Default action when a Warden check fails. 0=Log only, 1=Kick, 2=Ban. Individual checks can override this in the warden_checks table.

settings.label: Ban Duration
settings.desc: Seconds for Warden ban duration. 86400 = 24 hours. 0 = permanent ban. Only applies when penalty is set to ban.

settings.label: Debug Logging
settings.desc: Print detailed Warden check information to logs. Useful for troubleshooting false positives.

settings.label: Module Directory
settings.desc: Directory containing Warden scanning module files. Modules define what memory checks to perform on clients.

title: Movement Anticheat
description: Speed hack, fly hack, teleport, and movement exploit detection

settings.label: Enable Movement Anticheat
settings.desc: Master switch for player movement anticheat. Detects speedhacking, teleporting, fly hacking, wall climbing, and other movement cheats. Adds CPU overhead but essential for public servers.

settings.label: Players Only
settings.desc: Skip movement anticheat for GM accounts. 1 = GMs exempt (recommended, GMs often need to fly/teleport).

settings.label: Notify Cheaters
settings.desc: Send a message to the player when they fail an anticheat check. 0 = silent. 1 = notify. Notifying helps legitimate players understand disconnects.

settings.label: Ban Duration
settings.desc: Seconds for movement anticheat bans. 86400 = 24 hours. 0 = permanent. Applies when any individual check's penalty includes ban.

settings.label: Log Detailed Data
settings.desc: Save every anticheat tick result to the database for analysis. Generates massive data volume. Only enable for targeted debugging.

settings.label: Packet Log Size
settings.desc: Number of previous movement packets to dump to a file when a detection leads to kick/ban. Saved in .pkt format for WowPacketParser analysis. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Max Allowed Desync
settings.desc: Milliseconds of client-server time desync tolerated during movement extrapolation. 0 = strict.

title: GM Settings
description: Game Master behavior, visibility, tickets, and permissions

settings.label: GM Mode at Login
settings.desc: GM mode state at login. 0=Always off, 1=Always on, 2=Remember last state. GM mode enables special commands and bypasses many game restrictions.

settings.label: GM Visibility at Login
settings.desc: GM visibility at login. 0=Always invisible, 1=Always visible, 2=Remember last state. Invisible GMs can monitor without being seen by players.

settings.label: Accept Tickets at Login
settings.desc: GM ticket acceptance at login. 0=Don't accept, 1=Always accept, 2=Remember last state.

settings.label: GM Chat Badge
settings.desc: GM chat mode at login. When enabled, GM messages appear with a special tag/color. 0=Always off, 1=Always on, 2=Remember last state.

settings.label: GM Whisper Accept
settings.desc: GM whisper acceptance at login. 0=Block whispers, 1=Accept whispers, 2=Remember last state.

settings.label: GM List Min Level
settings.desc: Max GM level shown in the GM list when not in GM mode. 0=none, 1=mods, 2=GMs, 3=all staff.

settings.label: GM in /who Min Level
settings.desc: Max GM level shown in /who list. 0=only players, 3=all staff visible.

settings.label: Log GM Trades
settings.desc: Log GM trades and trade-slot enchanting to the GM log. Important for preventing GM item duplication abuse.

settings.label: GM Starting Level
settings.desc: Starting level for characters created by GM accounts. 1 = same as normal players.

settings.label: Allow Lower Security
settings.desc: Prevent lower security GMs from targeting higher security GMs with commands. 1 = enforced hierarchy.

settings.label: GM Kill Credit
settings.desc: Give loot, money, and reputation credit when using .die command on creatures. Useful for GM testing.

settings.label: GM Allow Trades
settings.desc: Allow GM accounts to trade, use mail, and auction house. 0 = block to prevent item injection into the economy.

settings.label: GM Public Channels
settings.desc: Allow GM characters to speak in General, Trade, and other public channels. 0 = GMs can't use public channels (encourages using GM tools instead). 1 = GMs can use public channels normally.

settings.label: Enable Ticket System
settings.desc: Allow players to open support tickets. 0 = ticket system disabled.

settings.label: Ticket Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum level to open a support ticket. Prevents level-1 spam accounts from flooding GM ticket queue.

settings.label: Ticket Admin Level
settings.desc: Security level required to assign tickets to the escalation queue. 7 = console only.

settings.label: God Mode Command
settings.desc: GM characters cannot die from damage (god mode). 0=disabled, 1=enabled. GMs can still die from environmental effects if they choose.

title: Visibility
description: View distances for continents, instances, battlegrounds, and flight

settings.label: Group Visibility Mode
settings.desc: Who can auto-detect invisible group members. 0=party only, 1=raid members, 2=same faction. Affects how stealth interacts with allied players.

settings.label: Continent Visibility
settings.desc: Yards at which players and creatures are visible on continent maps. Blizzlike ~90. Higher values let players see further but increase server CPU and bandwidth per player. Maximum limited by zone loading at ~333.

settings.label: Min Continent Visibility
settings.desc: Minimum visibility distance on continents when dynamic visibility reduction is active. Server won't reduce visibility below this value even under heavy load.

settings.label: Instance Visibility
settings.desc: Visibility distance inside instances/dungeons. Default ~120, higher than continents since instance populations are smaller. Blizzlike is around 120.

settings.label: Battleground Visibility
settings.desc: Visibility distance in battlegrounds. Default ~180, higher than continents because seeing enemies at range is important for PvP strategy.

settings.label: Flight Visibility
settings.desc: Visibility distance while on a flight path. 0 = show nothing while flying (saves bandwidth). Higher values let flying players see more ground detail.

settings.label: Grey Unit Distance
settings.desc: Grey distance buffer for fast-changing objects (players, creatures). Objects become invisible at distance + grey distance. Prevents rapid show/hide flickering at visibility boundary.

settings.label: Grey Object Distance
settings.desc: Grey distance buffer for slow-changing objects (game objects, corpses). Larger buffer because these don't need to update as quickly.

settings.label: Relocation Lower Limit
settings.desc: Yards a player must move before visibility is recalculated. Lower = more updates (smoother but more CPU). Higher = fewer updates.

settings.label: AI Relocation Notify Delay
settings.desc: Milliseconds between creature AI reactions to nearby player movement. Lower = more responsive AI but more CPU.

settings.label: Force Active Objects
settings.desc: Force creatures/objects with increased visibility range in templates to be always-active. 1 = respect template flags. 0 = ignore.

title: Rates & Multipliers
description: XP, drop rates, regeneration, reputation, skills, and death penalties

settings.label: Health Regen Rate
settings.desc: Health regeneration rate multiplier. 1 = Blizzlike. Higher values mean players heal faster out of combat.

settings.label: Mana Regen Rate
settings.desc: Mana regeneration rate multiplier. Higher values reduce downtime between pulls for casters.

settings.label: Rage Generation Rate
settings.desc: Rage gained from damage dealt/received. Higher values let warriors use abilities more frequently.

settings.label: Rage Decay Rate
settings.desc: Rate of rage decay out of combat. Lower values let warriors retain rage longer between fights.

settings.label: Focus Regen Rate
settings.desc: Focus regeneration rate for hunter pets.

settings.label: Pet Loyalty Rate
settings.desc: Pet loyalty gain rate. Higher values reduce the time to reach max loyalty.

settings.label: Energy Regen Rate
settings.desc: Rogue energy regeneration rate. Higher values increase rogue sustained damage.

settings.label: Money Drop Rate
settings.desc: Multiplier for money dropped by creatures. 1 = Blizzlike. Higher values cause gold inflation, making everything cheaper relative to mob grinding income.

settings.label: Kill XP Rate
settings.desc: XP gained from killing creatures. 1 = Blizzlike. 2 = double XP (reach max level roughly twice as fast). 5 = five times faster leveling. Setting to 0 disables kill XP entirely. Higher rates dramatically shorten the leveling journey.

settings.label: Elite Kill XP Rate
settings.desc: XP multiplier specifically for elite creature kills. Stacks with Rate.XP.Kill. Increasing this makes dungeon grinding more rewarding relative to questing.

settings.label: Quest XP Rate
settings.desc: XP gained from quest turn-ins. 1 = Blizzlike. Higher values make questing the dominant leveling strategy. Setting to 0 forces grinding.

settings.label: Exploration XP Rate
settings.desc: XP gained from discovering new areas. 1 = Blizzlike. Exploration XP is relatively small, so even high multipliers have modest impact on total leveling time.

settings.label: Personal XP Min Rate
settings.desc: Minimum personal XP rate (floor for server XP scaling). 1 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: Personal XP Max Rate
settings.desc: Maximum personal XP rate (cap for server XP scaling). 1 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: Rest XP In-Game
settings.desc: Rested XP accumulation rate while logged in (at inns/cities). 1 = Blizzlike. Higher values accumulate rest faster while playing.

settings.label: Rest XP Offline (Rest Area)
settings.desc: Rested XP accumulation rate while logged out at an inn or city. 1 = Blizzlike (8 hours for 1 bubble).

settings.label: Rest XP Offline (Wild)
settings.desc: Rested XP accumulation rate while logged out in the wilderness (non-inn). 1 = Blizzlike (1/4 the rate of inn rest).

settings.label: Fall Damage Rate
settings.desc: Fall damage multiplier. 1 = Blizzlike. 0 = no fall damage. 2 = double fall damage.

settings.label: Auction Duration Rate
settings.desc: Auction duration multiplier. 1 = Blizzlike (2/8/24 hour options). Lower values make auctions expire faster.

settings.label: Auction Deposit Rate
settings.desc: Auction deposit fee multiplier. Higher values make listing items more expensive, discouraging junk listings.

settings.label: Auction House Cut
settings.desc: Auction house cut (percentage of sale price). 1 = Blizzlike (5%). Higher multipliers increase the gold sink.

settings.label: Min Auction Deposit
settings.desc: Minimum auction deposit in copper. 0 = no minimum (deposit can be 0 copper).

settings.label: Max Auctions Per Account
settings.desc: Maximum concurrent auctions per account per auction house. 0 = unlimited. Limits AH market manipulation.

settings.label: Mining Yield Rate
settings.desc: Mining node use count multiplier. Higher values let nodes be mined more times before depleting.

settings.label: Mining Respawn Rate
settings.desc: Chance multiplier for mining node to have additional uses. Higher values mean nodes last longer.

settings.label: Talent Point Rate
settings.desc: Talent point gain rate. 1 = Blizzlike (one point per level 10-60, total 51). Higher values give more talent points per level, dramatically increasing player power.
settings.warn: Values above 1 allow more than 51 talent points, which may break game balance

settings.label: Respec Base Cost Rate
settings.desc: Base cost in gold for talent respec. 1 = Blizzlike (1 gold first respec).

settings.label: Respec Cost Multiplier
settings.desc: Cost multiplier per respec. 5 = Blizzlike (cost increases by 5g per respec: 1g, 5g, 10g, 15g...).

settings.label: Respec Max Cost Multiplier
settings.desc: Maximum respec cost multiplier. 10 = 50 gold cap (Blizzlike). Prevents respec cost from growing infinitely.

settings.label: Respec Min Cost Multiplier
settings.desc: Once a player reaches this multiplier tier, they can never drop below it even with decay. 2 = Blizzlike.

settings.label: Reputation Gain Rate
settings.desc: Reputation gain multiplier. 1 = Blizzlike. Higher values accelerate faction reputation grinds. Affects access to reputation-gated items, recipes, and enchants.

settings.label: Low Level Rep Kill Rate
settings.desc: Reputation from killing grey (trivial) creatures. 0.2 = 20% of normal rep (Blizzlike). Prevents easy rep farming on low-level mobs.

settings.label: Low Level Rep Quest Rate
settings.desc: Reputation from low-level quests. 1 = full rep regardless of quest level (Blizzlike).

settings.label: Instance Reset Time Rate
settings.desc: Instance reset timer multiplier. 1 = Blizzlike. 2 = double the days between resets. 0.5 = half the normal lockout time.

settings.label: Enable Durability Loss
settings.desc: Enable durability loss on death. When enabled, dying costs gold via repair bills. Disabling removes a major gold sink from the economy.

settings.label: Durability Loss Chance
settings.desc: Chance (0.0-1.0) of losing durability on equipped items when taking or dealing damage. 0.5 = 50% chance per hit. Affects ongoing repair costs during normal gameplay.

settings.label: Res Sickness Level
settings.desc: Level at which Resurrection Sickness starts applying (1 min base, +1 min per level above, up to 10 min). -10 = all characters get full 10 min sickness. maxlevel+1 = sickness never applies. Makes death more punishing at higher levels.

settings.label: PvP Corpse Reclaim Delay
settings.desc: Enable increasing corpse reclaim delay on repeated PvP deaths. Discourages zerging (dying and respawning to fight again immediately).

settings.label: PvE Corpse Reclaim Delay
settings.desc: Enable increasing corpse reclaim delay on repeated PvE deaths. Makes repeated wipes progressively more costly in time.

settings.label: Leave Bones (World)
settings.desc: Leave a skeleton at the death location after resurrection in the open world.

settings.label: Leave Bones (BG)
settings.desc: Leave skeletons at death locations in battlegrounds. Creates a visual history of battle.

settings.label: Corpse Cleanup Interval
settings.desc: Minutes between corpse cleanup passes. Removes old corpses from the world.

settings.label: Bones Expire Time
settings.desc: Minutes before skeleton bones despawn. 60 = 1 hour.

settings.label: Ghost Speed (World)
settings.desc: Ghost form run speed multiplier in the open world. 1.0 = normal. Higher values let ghosts reach corpses faster (reduces death penalty time).

settings.label: Ghost Speed (BG)
settings.desc: Ghost run speed in battlegrounds. Higher values get players back into the fight faster after dying.

settings.label: War Effort Completion Rate
settings.desc: AQ War Effort auto-completion rate per period. 0 = disabled (players must turn in all resources). Non-zero values gradually complete the war effort automatically.

settings.label: War Effort Period
settings.desc: Seconds between auto-completion ticks for the AQ War Effort. 86400 = once per day.

settings.label: PvP Pool Size Per Faction
settings.desc: Override the PvP honor pool size per faction. 0 = use actual player count. On low-pop servers, Rank 14 is mathematically impossible below ~167 players. Setting this to 167+ makes all ranks achievable regardless of population.
settings.warn: Must be 167+ for Rank 14 to be mathematically achievable on low-pop servers

title: Battlegrounds
description: BG queues, deserter, premades, and Alterac Valley

settings.label: Cast Deserter
settings.desc: Apply the Deserter debuff (15 min BG queue lockout) to players who leave a battleground in progress. Discourages queue-dodging and rage-quitting.

settings.label: Announce BG Queue Join
settings.desc: Announce BG queue joins. 0=off, 1=to joined player only, 2=server-wide. Server-wide helps low-pop servers encourage BG participation.

settings.label: Announce BG Start
settings.desc: Post a chat announcement when a battleground match starts.

settings.label: BG Invitation Type
settings.desc: BG invitation strategy. 0=Normal (fill as fast as possible). 1=Balanced (try to match faction numbers). Balanced provides fairer games.

settings.label: Premature End Timer
settings.desc: Milliseconds before ending a BG with too few players on one side. 300000 = 5 minutes. 0 = disabled (not recommended).

settings.label: Premade Wait for Premade
settings.desc: Milliseconds premade groups wait for another premade opponent before being matched normally. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Premade Min Group Size
settings.desc: Minimum group size to be treated as a premade for queue matching purposes.

settings.label: BG Queue Slots
settings.desc: Number of BG queue slots players get. 0 = auto (patch-based). Players can queue for this many BGs simultaneously.

settings.label: PvP Flag in BGs
settings.desc: Allow PvP flagging inside battlegrounds.

settings.label: Randomize Queues
settings.desc: Randomize queue positions instead of first-in-first-out. Can prevent queue gaming.

settings.label: Max Group Queue Size
settings.desc: Maximum group size for BG queue. Groups larger than this are queued individually.

settings.label: AV Min Queue Players
settings.desc: Minimum players queued per faction before Alterac Valley can start. 0 = use database default (typically 20).

settings.label: AV Max Players
settings.desc: Initial max players per side in AV. 0 = use database default (40). Should satisfy: InitMaxPlayers * 2 > MinPlayersInQueue.

title: Outdoor PvP & LFG
description: World PvP objectives and Looking For Group matchmaking

settings.label: Silithus PvP
settings.desc: Enable Silithus outdoor PvP objectives (sand/dust collection). Part of the Silithus world PvP system.

settings.label: Eastern Plaguelands PvP
settings.desc: Enable Eastern Plaguelands tower capture outdoor PvP. Capturing towers grants zone-wide buffs.

settings.label: LFG Auto-Matchmaking
settings.desc: Enable automatic talent-based role detection for LFG matchmaking. 0 = use class-based roles (warrior=tank). 1 = check talent tree for role assignment.

settings.label: LFG Match Timer
settings.desc: Seconds in LFG queue before matchmaking stops trying to find a perfect match and relaxes criteria.

title: Network
description: Networking threads, TCP settings, and packet handling

settings.label: Network I/O Threads
settings.desc: Number of network I/O threads. Recommended 1 thread per ~1000 concurrent connections. For a small private server, 1 is sufficient. Increase for high-pop servers.

settings.label: TCP Send Buffer
settings.desc: TCP send buffer size (SO_SNDBUF). -1 = use OS default. Increase for high-bandwidth servers with many players.

settings.label: TCP No Delay
settings.desc: Disable Nagle's algorithm (TCP_NODELAY). 0=Enabled (batches small packets, saves bandwidth, adds ~200ms latency). 1=Disabled (sends packets immediately, lower latency, more bandwidth). For gaming, 1 is usually preferred.

settings.label: Kick on Bad Packet
settings.desc: Kick clients that send malformed packets. 0 = ignore bad packets. 1 = kick. May kick legitimate players with buggy addons.

settings.label: Packet Broadcast Threads
settings.desc: Threads for broadcasting update packets to nearby players. 0 = broadcast on main thread. Increase for better performance in crowded areas.

settings.label: Broadcast Frequency
settings.desc: Milliseconds between packet broadcast cycles. Lower = more responsive but more CPU. 50 = 20 broadcasts/second.

settings.label: Reduce Vis on High Diff
settings.desc: Reduce visibility distance when broadcast diff exceeds this (ms). 0 = disabled. Automatic performance scaling under load.

settings.label: Trusted Proxy Servers
settings.desc: Comma-separated IPs of trusted Proxy Protocol v2 servers. Enables real IP detection behind load balancers. Empty = no proxies.

settings.label: Auth Timeout
settings.desc: Seconds before kicking a connection that hasn't authenticated. 0 = disabled. Protects against connection flooding attacks.

title: Console, RA & SOAP
description: Remote administration and SOAP API access

settings.label: Enable Console
settings.desc: Enable the interactive server console for typing commands directly. Disable if running headless with RA/SOAP only.

settings.label: Enable RA
settings.desc: Enable the Remote Administration (RA) TCP interface. Required for MangosSuperUI to send commands to the server. RA allows sending any server command remotely.
settings.warn: Must be enabled for MangosSuperUI to function. Ensure proper firewall rules.

settings.label: RA Listen IP
settings.desc: IP address the RA interface listens on. 0.0.0.0 = accept connections from any IP. For security, consider binding to 127.0.0.1 (localhost only) if MangosSuperUI runs on the same machine.

settings.label: RA Port
settings.desc: TCP port for Remote Administration connections. Must match the port configured in MangosSuperUI's appsettings.json.

settings.label: RA Min Account Level
settings.desc: Minimum account security level required to connect via RA. 3 = GM level. Higher values restrict RA to senior staff. 0 = any account can connect (dangerous).
settings.warn: Setting below 3 allows lower-privilege accounts to run server commands remotely

settings.label: RA Restricted Mode
settings.desc: Restrict RA from running console-only (SEC_CONSOLE) commands. 1 = some commands are console-only. 0 = RA can run all commands.

settings.label: Enable SOAP
settings.desc: Enable the SOAP XML web service API. Allows external applications to send commands and query server state via HTTP. Alternative to RA for web integrations.

settings.label: SOAP Listen IP
settings.desc: IP address for SOAP listener. 127.0.0.1 = localhost only (secure). 0.0.0.0 = accept from anywhere (requires firewall protection).

settings.label: SOAP Port
settings.desc: TCP port for SOAP service.

title: Character Deletion
description: Character deletion method and recovery

settings.label: Deletion Method
settings.desc: How deleted characters are handled. 0=Permanent removal from database (unrecoverable). 1=Soft-delete (character is unlinked from account, name freed, can be restored by admin). Soft-delete is safer.

settings.label: Soft Delete Min Level
settings.desc: Minimum level for the configured deletion method (soft-delete). Characters below this level are always permanently deleted regardless of Method setting.

settings.label: Soft Delete Keep Days
settings.desc: Days to keep soft-deleted characters before permanent removal. 0 = keep forever (never auto-purge). Only applies when Method=1.

title: Auction House Bot
description: Automated auction house population

settings.label: Enable AH Bot
settings.desc: Enable the automatic auction house bot. Populates the AH with items based on the auctionhousebot database table. Essential for low-pop servers to have a functional economy.

settings.label: AH Faction ID
settings.desc: Faction template ID for AH bot listings. 120=Neutral (Booty Bay/Gadgetzan/Everlook), 55=Alliance (IF/SW/Darnassus), 29=Horde (Org/TB/UC). Determines which auction house the bot populates.

settings.label: Item Count
settings.desc: Maximum total listings the AH bot maintains. Includes both bot and player listings. 50 is very sparse — set to 500-2000 for a populated-feeling auction house.

title: Player Bots
description: Random bots, party bots, and battle bots

settings.label: Enable Random Bots
settings.desc: Spawn random AI bots that wander the world. These bots have NO AI by default — you need to code behavior and assign it. They exist purely as visual population filler.

settings.label: Min Random Bots
settings.desc: Minimum number of random bots the server tries to maintain. Server will spawn bots until this count is reached.

settings.label: Max Random Bots
settings.desc: Maximum random bots allowed. Server won't spawn beyond this count. Higher values increase CPU and memory usage.

settings.label: Bot Refresh Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between checks for random bot population. Server checks if enough bots are spawned and adds more if needed.

settings.label: Save Bot State
settings.desc: Save playerbot character progress to database. 0 = bots reset on restart. 1 = bot progress persists.

settings.label: Bot Debug Output
settings.desc: Print additional bot AI debug information to the console. Very verbose.

settings.label: Bot AI Update Interval
settings.desc: Milliseconds between bot AI updates. 1000 = 1 second (bots react every second). Lower values make bots more responsive but cost more CPU. With 150 bots, this is a major performance knob.

settings.label: Show Bots in /who
settings.desc: Show bot characters in /who results. Enabling makes bots appear as real players in /who, adding to perceived population. Disabling keeps them hidden from player queries.

settings.label: Max Party Bots
settings.desc: Maximum party bots a normal player can summon. 0 = unlimited (no cap). Party bots fill group slots and let solo players attempt group content.

settings.label: Skip Party Bot Checks
settings.desc: Skip restriction checks when players summon party bots. 0 = enforce restrictions. 1 = no restrictions (bots can be summoned anywhere).

settings.label: Party Bot Auto-Equip
settings.desc: How party bots get their gear. 0=Normal starting items (weak), 1=Random level-appropriate items, 2=Premade gear template. Random gear makes bots viable for their level range.

settings.label: Party Bot Gear Level Diff
settings.desc: Item level range for random gear. Bots equip random items within this many levels of their character level. Lower = better gear. Only applies with AutoEquip=1.

settings.label: Battle Bot Auto-Equip
settings.desc: Battle bot gear mode. 0=Starting items, 1=Random level-appropriate, 2=Premade template.

settings.label: Battle Bot Auto-Join
settings.desc: Automatically add enough battle bots to start a BG when a player queues. Essential for low-pop servers where BGs would never start otherwise.

title: Mail Spam Prevention
description: Mail restrictions for spam prevention

settings.label: Spam Mail Expiry
settings.desc: Auto-expire mails from spam-flagged accounts after this many seconds. 0 = disabled (mails persist normally). Part of the mail spam prevention system.

settings.label: Max Spam Mails
settings.desc: Maximum mails a spam-restricted account can send. Low values prevent mass gold-selling spam mail.

settings.label: Spam Restriction Level
settings.desc: Character level below which mail spam restrictions apply. Characters above this level are unrestricted.

settings.label: Spam Mail Money Limit
settings.desc: Maximum copper that spam-restricted accounts can attach to mail. 0 = no restriction on gold attachments.

settings.label: Spam Mail Item Limit
settings.desc: Maximum items spam-restricted accounts can attach to mail. 0 = no restriction on item attachments.

title: Dynamic Respawn
description: Scale creature respawn times based on nearby players

settings.label: Detection Range
settings.desc: Yards around spawn point to count nearby players for dynamic respawn scaling. -1 = feature completely disabled. When enabled, creatures respawn faster when more players are in the area, reducing competition.

settings.label: Reduction Per Player
settings.desc: Percentage reduction in respawn time per nearby player. Example: 10 means each nearby player reduces respawn time by 10%. With 5 players, respawns are 50% faster.

settings.label: Max Reduction Rate
settings.desc: Maximum respawn time reduction as a fraction (0.0-1.0). 0.5 = respawns can be reduced by up to 50%. 0 = disabled.

settings.label: Min Respawn Time
settings.desc: Minimum respawn time floor in seconds. Dynamic scaling will never reduce respawn time below this value. 0 = no floor.

settings.label: Affect Respawn Below
settings.desc: Only apply dynamic respawn to creatures with base respawn time below this (seconds). 0 = affect all creatures regardless of respawn time.

settings.label: Affect Level Below
settings.desc: Only apply dynamic respawn to creatures below this level. 0 = affect all levels.

settings.label: Players Threshold
settings.desc: Minimum players in detection range before dynamic respawn activates. 0 = always active when enabled.

settings.label: Player Level Diff
settings.desc: Maximum level difference between creature and player for the player to count toward dynamic respawn. 0 = any level counts. Prevents high-level players from accelerating respawns in low-level areas.
